content:
Overall I enjoyed the anniversary but as others have said the raid format felt unnecessarily restrictive and stressful.

I also started the game only just a couple of patches before the anni patch, so I wasn't even able to do the theoretical max of 17 boss fights per day, iirc because of the level sync and rotating rerun banners i was able to get to about 14~15, but even not doing the max amount of runs a day I felt like I was burning out near the end of the event.

As for a positive of the raid event, all awakeners being level-synced was huge for me as a new player as I got a chance to try many teams and strategies that I probably wouldn't have been able to try until much later in the game after I'd accumulated more resources to build them.

Aside from that I really appreciated the story catchup campaign offering 200 rolls, that was a huge boost to me especially being new and especially while the rerun banner rotation was happening. But also, for a new player catching up to the entire story up to the latest chapter hoping to claim all those rewards was _very_ stressful lol. It's not something that I'll have to go through again now that I'm caught up and can pace myself with the release schedule, but I think new players have to make a choice between stressing themselves out about it or just not caring and just settling for whatever they are able to obtain. I can understand wanting to encourage players that are behind to catch up with the story, but especially as the game continues to add more story and the difficulty also continues to ramp up.. it might be a good idea to extend the duration of the campaign so that new players have a bit more breathing room.
